teamStates,\\n155:       userState,\\n156:       orgAutoOptIn,\\n157:       teamAutoOptIns,\\n158:       userAutoOptIn,\\n159:     });\\n160: \\n161:     // Get the policy for this feature from the teamStatesById[orgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n39:   }\n40:   return \"inherit\";\n41: }\n42: \n43: function getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId: number | null, teamsAutoOptIn: Record<number, boolean>): boolean {\n44:   if (orgId !== null) {\n45:     return teamsAutoOptIn[orgId] ?? false;\n46:   }\n47:   return false;\n48: }\n49: \n50: function getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId: number, parentOrgId: number | null | undefined): number[] {\n51:   if (parentOrgId) {\n52:     return [teamId, parentOrgId];\n53:   }\n54:   return [teamId];\n55: }\n56: \n57: function getOrgStateForTeam(\n58:   parentOrgId: number | null | undefined,\n59:   teamStates: Record<string, Record<number, FeatureState>>,\n60:   slug: FeatureId\n61: ): FeatureState {\n62:   if (parentOrgId) {\n63:     return teamStates[slug]?.[parentOrgId] ?? \"inherit\";\n64:   }\n65:   return \"inherit\";\n66: }\n67: \n68: /**\n69:  * Service class for managing feature opt-in logic.\n70:  * Computes effective states based on global, org, team, and user settings.\n71:  */\n72: export class FeatureOptInService implements IFeatureOptInService {\n73:   constructor(private featuresRepository: FeaturesRepository) {}\n74: \n75:   /**\n76:    * Core method: Resolve feature states for a user across all their teams.\n77:    *\n78:    * Precedence rules:\n79:    * 1. Global disabled → false (stop)\n80:    * 2. Org explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n81:    * 3. Org explicitly enabled → allowed at org level, continue to teams\n82:    * 4. Org inherits (or no org) → check teams\n83:    * 5. All teams explicitly disabled → false (stop)\n84:    * 6. At least one team enabled OR inherits → allowed at team level, continue to user\n85:    * 7. User explicitly disabled → false\n86:    * 8. User explicitly enabled OR inherits → true\n87:    *\n88:    * Auto-opt-in transformation:\n89:    * - If autoOptInFeatures=true at a level AND state is \"inherit\", transform to \"enabled\"\n90:    * - This transformation happens before computing effectiveEnabled\n91:    */\n92:   async resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n93:     userId,\n94:     orgId,\n95:     teamIds,\n96:     featureIds,\n97:   }: {\n98:     userId: number;\n99:     orgId: number | null;\n100:     teamIds: number[];\n101:     featureIds: FeatureId[];\n102:   }): Promise<Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState>> {\n103:     const allTeamIds = orgId !== null ? [orgId, ...teamIds] : teamIds;\n104: \n105:     const [allFeatures, allTeamStates, userStates, userAutoOptIn, teamsAutoOptIn] = await Promise.all([\n106:       this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n107:       this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({ teamIds: allTeamIds, featureIds }),\n108:       this.featuresRepository.getUserFeatureStates({ userId, featureIds }),\n109:       this.featuresRepository.getUserAutoOptIn(userId),\n110:       this.featuresRepository.getTeamsAutoOptIn(allTeamIds),\n111:     ]);\n112: \n113:     const globalEnabledMap = new Map(allFeatures.map((feature) => [feature.slug, feature.enabled ?? false]));\n114:     const resolvedStates: Record<string, ResolvedFeatureState> = {};\n115: \n116:     for (const featureId of featureIds) {\n117:       const state = this.resolveFeatureState(\n118:         featureId,\n119:         orgId,\n120:         teamIds,\n121:         globalEnabledMap,\n122:         allTeamStates,\n123:         userStates,\n124:         teamsAutoOptIn,\n125:         userAutoOptIn\n126:       );\n127:       resolvedStates[featureId] = state;\n128:     }\n129: \n130:     return resolvedStates;\n131:   }\n132: \n133:   private resolveFeatureState(\n134:     featureId: FeatureId,\n135:     orgId: number | null,\n136:     teamIds: number[],\n137:     globalEnabledMap: boolean>,\n141:     userAutoOptIn: boolean\n142:   ): ResolvedFeatureState {\n143:     const globalEnabled = globalEnabledMap.get(featureId) ?? false;\n144:     const teamStatesById =     const userState = userStates[featureId] ?? \"inherit\";\n149:     const orgAutoOptIn = getOrgAutoOptIn(orgId, teamsAutoOptIn);\n150:     const teamAutoOptIns = teamIds.map((teamId) => teamsAutoOptIn[teamId] ?? false);\n151: \n152:     const { effectiveOrgState, effectiveTeamStates, effectiveUserState } = applyAutoOptIn({\n153:       orgState,\n154:       teamStates,\n155:       userState,\n156:       orgAutoOptIn,\n157:       teamAutoOptIns,\n158:       userAutoOptIn,\n159:     });\n160: \n161:     // Get the policy for this feature from the config\n162:     const featureConfig = getOptInFeatureConfig(featureId);\n163:     const policy: OptInFeaturePolicy = featureConfig?.policy ?? \"permissive\";\n164: \n165:     const { enabled: effectiveEnabled, reason: effectiveReason } = computeEffectiveStateAcrossTeams({\n166:       globalEnabled,\n167:       orgState: effectiveOrgState,\n168:       teamStates: effectiveTeamStates,\n169:       userState: effectiveUserState,\n170:       policy,\n171:     });\n172: \n173:     return {\n174:       featureId,\n175:       globalEnabled,\n176:       orgState,\n177:       teamStates,\n178:       userState,\n179:       effectiveEnabled,\n180:       effectiveReason,\n181:       orgAutoOptIn,\n182:       teamAutoOptIns,\n183:       userAutoOptIn,\n184:     };\n185:   }\n186: \n187:   /**\n188:    * List all opt-in features with their states for a user across teams.\n189:    * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to \"user\".\n190:    */\n191:   async listFeaturesForUser(input: {\n192:     userId: number;\n193:     orgId: number | null;\n194:     teamIds: number[];\n195:   }): Promise<ListFeaturesForUserResult[]> {\n196:     const { userId, orgId, teamIds } = input;\n197:     const userScopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(\"user\");\n198:     const featureIds = userScopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug);\n199: \n200:     const resolvedStates = await this.resolveFeatureStatesAcrossTeams({\n201:       userId,\n202:       orgId,\n203:       teamIds,\n204:       featureIds,\n205:     });\n206: \n207:     return featureIds.map((featureId) => resolvedStates[featureId]).filter((state) => state.globalEnabled);\n208:   }\n209: \n210:   /**\n211:    * List all opt-in features with their raw states for a team or organization.\n212:    * Used for team/org admin settings page to configure feature opt-in.\n213:    * Only returns features that are in the allowlist, globally enabled, and scoped to the specified scope.\n214:    * If parentOrgId is provided, also returns the organization state for each feature.\n215:    */\n216:   async listFeaturesForTeam(input: {\n217:     teamId: number;\n218:     parentOrgId?: number | null;\n219:     scope?: OptInFeatureScope;\n220:   }): Promise<ListFeaturesForTeamResult[]> {\n221:     const { teamId, parentOrgId, scope = \"team\" } = input;\n222:     const teamIdsToQuery = getTeamIdsToQuery(teamId, parentOrgId);\n223:     const scopedFeatures = getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope);\n224: \n225:     const [allFeatures, teamStates] = await Promise.all([\n226:       this.featuresRepository.getAllFeatures(),\n227:       this.featuresRepository.getTeamsFeatureStates({\n228:         teamIds: teamIdsToQuery,\n229:         featureIds: scopedFeatures.map((config) => config.slug),\n230:       }),\n231:     ]);\n232: \n233:     const results = scopedFeatures.map((config) => {\n234:       const globalFeature = allFeatures.find((f) => f.slug === config.slug);\n235:       const globalEnabled = globalFeature?.enabled ?? false;\n236:       const teamState = teamStates[config.slug]?.[teamId] ?? \"inherit\";\n237:       const orgState = getOrgStateForTeam(parentOrgId, teamStates, config.slug);\n238: \n239:       return { featureId: config.slug, globalEnabled, teamState, orgState };\n240:     });\n241: \n242:     return results.filter((result) => result.globalEnabled);\n243:   }\n244: \n245:   /**\n246:    * Set user's feature state.\n247:    * Delegates to FeaturesRepository.setUserFeatureState.\n248:    * Throws an const { teamId, featureId, state } = input;\n288:     const scope = input.scope ?? \"team\";\n289: \n290:     if (!isFeatureAllowedForScope(featureId, scope)) {\n291:       throw new Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\\n10:   scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\\n11: }\\n12: \\n13: /** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\\n14: export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\\\"org\\\", \\\"team\\\", \\\"user\\\"];\\n15: \\n16: /**\\n17:  * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\\n18:  * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\\n19:  */\\n20: export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\\n21:   // Example - to be populated with actual features\\n22:   // {\\n23:   //   slug: \\\"bookings-v3\\\",\\n24:   // function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\\n35:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\\n36: }\\n37: \\n38: /**\\n39:  * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\\n40:  * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\\n41:  */\\n42: export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\\n43:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: /**\\n47:  * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\\n48:  */\\n49: export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\\n50: \\n51: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\\n52: export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"user\\\"));\\n53: \\n54: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\\n55: export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"team\\\"));\\n56: \\n57: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\\n58: export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"org\\\"));\\n59: \\n60: /**\\n61:  * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\\n62:  * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\\n63:  */\\n64: export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\\n65:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: /**\\n69:  * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\\n70:  * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\\n71:  * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\\n72:  */\\n73: export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\\n74:   const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\\n75:   if (!config) return false;\\n76:   return !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\\n77: }\\n78: import type { FeatureId } from \\\"@calcom/features/flags/config\\\";\\n2: import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \\\"./types\\\";\\n3: \\n4: export interface OptInFeatureConfig {\\n5:   slug: FeatureId;\\n6:   titleI18nKey: string;\\n7:   descriptionI18nKey: string;\\n8:   policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\\n9:   /** Scopes where this feature can be configured. Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\\n10:   scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\\n11: }\\n12: \\n13: /** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\\n14: export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\\\"org\\\", \\\"team\\\", \\\"user\\\"];\\n15: \\n16: /**\\n17:  * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\\n18:  * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\\n19:  */\\n20: export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\\n21:   // Example - to be populated with actual features\\n22:   // {\\n23:   //   slug: \\\"bookings-v3\\\",\\n24:   //   titleI18nKey: \\\"bookings_v3_title\\\",\\n25:   //   descriptionI18nKey: \\\"bookings_v3_description\\\",\\n26:   //   policy: \\\"permissive\\\",\\n27:   //   scope: [\\\"org\\\", \\\"team\\\", \\\"user\\\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\\n28:   // },\\n29: ];\\n30: \\n31: /**\\n32:  * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\\n33:  */\\n34: export function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\\n35:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\\n36: }\\n37: \\n38: /**\\n39:  * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\\n40:  * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\\n41:  */\\n42: export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\\n43:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: /**\\n47:  * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\\n48:  */\\n49: export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\\n50: \\n51: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\\n52: export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"user\\\"));\\n53: \\n54: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\\n55: export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"team\\\"));\\n56: \\n57: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\\n58: export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\\\"org\\\"));\\n59: \\n60: /**\\n61:  * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\\n62:  * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\\n63:  */\\n64: export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\\n65:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: /**\\n69:  * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\\n70:  * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\\n71:  * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\\n72:  */\\n73: export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\\n74:   const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\\n75:   if (!config) return false;\\n76:   return !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\\n77: }\\n78: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cal.com--4b99072b@4b99072bcc01e7a252df828db71248a202cb65d1/-/blob/packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: import type { FeatureId } from \"@calcom/features/flags/config\";\n2: import type { OptInFeaturePolicy, OptInFeatureScope } from \"./types\";\n3: \n4: export interface OptInFeatureConfig {\n5:   slug: FeatureId;\n6:   titleI18nKey: string;\n7:   descriptionI18nKey: string;\n8:   policy: OptInFeaturePolicy;\n9:   /** Scopes where this feature can be configured. Defaults to all scopes if not specified. */\n10:   scope?: OptInFeatureScope[];\n11: }\n12: \n13: /** All available scopes for feature opt-in configuration */\n14: export const ALL_SCOPES: OptInFeatureScope[] = [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"];\n15: \n16: /**\n17:  * Features that appear in opt-in settings.\n18:  * Add new features here to make them available for user/team opt-in.\n19:  */\n20: export const OPT_IN_FEATURES: OptInFeatureConfig[] = [\n21:   // Example - to be populated with actual features\n22:   // {\n23:   //   slug: \"bookings-v3\",\n24:   //   titleI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_title\",\n25:   //   descriptionI18nKey: \"bookings_v3_description\",\n26:   //   policy: \"permissive\",\n27:   //   scope: [\"org\", \"team\", \"user\"], // Optional: defaults to all scopes if not specified\n28:   // },\n29: ];\n30: \n31: /**\n32:  * Get the configuration for a specific opt-in feature by slug.\n33:  */\n34: export function getOptInFeatureConfig(slug: string): OptInFeatureConfig | undefined {\n35:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.find((f) => f.slug === slug);\n36: }\n37: \n38: /**\n39:  * Check if a slug is in the opt-in allowlist.\n40:  * Acts as a type guard, narrowing the slug to FeatureId when true.\n41:  */\n42: export function isOptInFeature(slug: string): slug is FeatureId {\n43:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => f.slug === slug);\n44: }\n45: \n46: /**\n47:  * Check if there are any opt-in features available.\n48:  */\n49: export const HAS_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.length > 0;\n50: \n51: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the user scope */\n52: export const HAS_USER_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"user\"));\n53: \n54: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the team scope */\n55: export const HAS_TEAM_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"team\"));\n56: \n57: /** Whether there are opt-in features available for the org scope */\n58: export const HAS_ORG_OPT_IN_FEATURES: boolean = OPT_IN_FEATURES.some((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(\"org\"));\n59: \n60: /**\n61:  * Get opt-in features that are available for a specific scope.\n62:  * Features without a scope field are available for all scopes.\n63:  */\n64: export function getOptInFeaturesForScope(scope: OptInFeatureScope): OptInFeatureConfig[] {\n65:   return OPT_IN_FEATURES.filter((f) => !f.scope || f.scope.includes(scope));\n66: }\n67: \n68: /**\n69:  * Check if a feature is allowed for a specific scope.\n70:  * Features without a scope field are allowed for all scopes.\n71:  * Features not in the config are NOT allowed (must be explicitly configured).\n72:  */\n73: export function isFeatureAllowedForScope(slug: string, scope: OptInFeatureScope): boolean {\n74:   const config = getOptInFeatureConfig(slug);\n75:   if (!config) return false;\n76:   return !config.scope || config.scope.includes(scope);\n77: }\n78: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/cal.com--4b99072b@4b99072bcc01e7a252df828db71248a202cb65d1/-/blob/packages/features/feature-opt-in/config.ts"}}}

import { z } from \\\"zod\\\";\\n3: \\n4: import { getFeatureOptInService } from \\\"@calcom/features/di/containers/FeatureOptInService\\\";\\n5: import { TeamRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/ee/teams/repositories/TeamRepository\\\";\\n6: import { isOptInFeature } from \\\"@calcom/features/feature-opt-in/config\\\";\\n7: import { FeaturesRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/flags/features.repository\\\";\\n8: import { MembershipRepository } from \\\"@calcom/features/membership/repositories/MembershipRepository\\\";\\n9: import { prisma } from \\\"@calcom/prisma\\\";\\n10: \\n11: import { TRPCError } from \\\"@trpc/server\\\";\\n12: \\n13: import authedProcedure from \\\"../../../procedures/authedProcedure\\\";\\n14: import { router } from \\\"../../../trpc\\\";\\n15: import { createOrgPbacProcedure, createTeamPbacProcedure } from \\\"../../../procedures/pbacProcedures\\\";\\n16: \\n17: const featureStateSchema: ZodEnum<[\\\"enabled\\\", \\\"disabled\\\", \\\"inherit\\\"]> = z.enum([\\\"enabled\\\", \\\"disabled\\\", \\\"inherit\\\"]);\\n18: \\n19: const featureOptInService = getFeatureOptInService();\\n20: const featuresRepository = new FeaturesRepository(prisma);\\n21: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\\n22: \\n23: /**\\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\\n26:  */\\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\\n28:   const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\\n29:     userId,\\n30:     filters: { accepted: true },\\n31:   });\\n32: \\n33:   let orgId: number | null = null;\\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\\n35: \\n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\\n39:     } else {\\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\\n41:     }\\n42:   }\\n43: \\n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\\n48:   /**\\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\\n50:    * This considers all teams/orgs the user belongs to.\\n51:    */\\n52:   listForUser: authedProcedure.query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n53:     const { orgId, teamIds } = await getUserOrgAndTeamIds(ctx.user.id);\\n54: \\n55:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForUser({\\n56:       userId: ctx.user.id,\\n57:       orgId,\\n58:       teamIds,\\n59:     });\\n60:   }),\\n61: \\n62:   /**\\n63:    * Get all opt-in features with states for a team settings page.\\n64:    * Used by team admins to configure feature opt-in for their team.\\n65:    * Also returns the organization state if the team belongs to an organization.\\n66:    */\\n67:   listForTeam: createTeamPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ input }) => {\\n68:     // Get the team's parent organization ID (if any)\\n69:     const parentOrg = await teamRepository.findParentOrganizationByTeamId(input.teamId);\\n70:     const parentOrgId = parentOrg?.id ?? null;\\n71: \\n72:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: input.teamId, parentOrgId, scope: \\\"team\\\" });\\n73:   }),\\n74: \\n75:   /**\\n76:    * Get all opt-in features with states for organization settings page.\\n77:    * Used by org admins to configure feature opt-in for their organization.\\n78:    */\\n79:   listForOrganization: createOrgPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.read\\\").query(async ({ ctx }) => {\\n80:     // Organizations use the same listFeaturesForTeam since they're stored in TeamFeatures\\n81:     // Pass scope: \\\"org\\\" to filter features that are scoped to organizations\\n82:     return featureOptInService.listFeaturesForTeam({ teamId: ctx.organizationId, scope: \\\"org\\\" });\\n83:   }),\\n84: \\n85:   /**\\n86:    * Set user's feature state.\\n87:    */\\n88:   setUserState: authedProcedure\\n89:     .input(\\n90:       z.object({\\n91:         slug: z.string(),\\n92:         state: featureStateSchema,\\n93:       })\\n94:     )\\n95:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n96:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n97:         throw new TRPCError({\\n98:           code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n99:           message: \\\"Invalid slug. This feature is not opt-in configurable.\\\",\\n100:         });\\n101:       }\\n102: \\n103:       await featureOptInService.setUserFeatureState({\\n104:         userId: ctx.user.id,\\n105:         featureId: input.slug,\\n106:         state: input.state,\\n107:         assignedBy: ctx.user.id,\\n108:       });\\n109: \\n110:       return { success: true };\\n111:     }),\\n112: \\n113:   /**\\n114:    * Set team's feature state (requires team admin).\\n115:    */\\n116:   setTeamState: createTeamPbacProcedure(\\\"featureOptIn.update\\\")\\n117:     .input(\\n118:       z.object({\\n119:         slug: z.string(),\\n120:         state: featureStateSchema,\\n121:       })\\n122:     )\\n123:     .mutation(async ({ ctx, input }) => {\\n124:       if (!isOptInFeature(input.slug)) {\\n125:         throw new TRPCError({\\n126:           code: \\\"BAD_REQUEST\\\",\\n127:           message: \\\"Invalid slug. autoOptIn };\\n178:   }),\\n179: \\n180:   /**\\n181:    * Set user's auto opt-in preference.\\n182:    */\\n183:   setUserAutoOptIn: authedProcedure\\n184:     .input(\\n185:       z.object({\\n186: \"inherit\"]> = z.enum([\"enabled\", \"disabled\", \"inherit\"]);\n18: \n19: const featureOptInService = getFeatureOptInService();\n20: const featuresRepository = new FeaturesRepository(prisma);\n21: const teamRepository = new TeamRepository(prisma);\n22: \n23: /**\n24:  * Helper to get user's org and team IDs from their memberships.\n25:  * Returns orgId (if user belongs to an org) and teamIds (non-org teams).\n26:  */\n27: async function getUserOrgAndTeamIds(userId: number): Promise<{ orgId: number | null; teamIds: number[] }> {\n28:   const memberships = await MembershipRepository.findAllByUserId({\n29:     userId,\n30:     filters: { accepted: true },\n31:   });\n32: \n33:   let orgId: number | null = null;\n34:   const teamIds: number[] = [];\n35: \n36:   for (const membership of memberships) {\n37:     if (membership.team.isOrganization) {\n38:       orgId = membership.teamId;\n39:     } else {\n40:       teamIds.push(membership.teamId);\n41:     }\n42:   }\n43: \n44:   return { orgId, teamIds };\n45: }\n46: \n47: export const featureOptInRouter = router({\n48:   /**\n49:    * Get all opt-in features with states for current user.\n50:    * Unconditionally passing it violates the contract.\n\n### High-Severity Compliance Violations (3)\n\n4. **setUserState (line 96)** - Missing scope validation for \"user\" level\n5. **setTeamState (line 124)** - Missing scope validation for \"team\" level\n6. **setOrganizationState (line 153)** - Missing scope validation for \"org\" level\n\n**Root Cause:** The router validates that slugs exist in the opt-in feature allowlist but doesn't use `isFeatureAllowedForScope()` to validate the correct scope.
